feature: Create xAI and OpenRouter AI Providers
workflow_rationale: This is a new feature implementation that adds two new provider packages to the existing AI provider system. Both providers follow the established OpenAIProvider pattern and can be developed in parallel as they are independent packages.

phases.name: XAI Package Setup
phases.description: Create the @clippyjs/ai-xai package structure with all configuration files

subtasks.description: Create package.json for ai-xai with correct dependencies and scripts
subtasks.notes: Created packages/ai-xai/package.json following ai-openai pattern. Uses openai npm package (^6.8.1) for OpenAI-compatible xAI API. Verification passed: jq '.name' returns "@clippyjs/ai-xai".

Created tsconfig.json for ai-xai package following the exact pattern from ai-openai/tsconfig.json. Extends tsconfig.react.json, references types and ai packages, includes src/**/* and excludes node_modules and dist.

subtasks.description: Create rollup.config.js for ai-xai build configuration
subtasks.notes: Created rollup.config.js for ai-xai following the exact pattern from ai-openai. Configuration includes CJS and ESM outputs, external dependencies (@clippyjs/ai, openai), and standard plugins (typescript, resolve, commonjs, terser, replace).

subtasks.description: Create vitest.config.ts for ai-xai test configuration
subtasks.notes: Created vitest.config.ts for ai-xai package following the exact pattern from ai-openai. File verified to exist.

phases.name: OpenRouter Package Setup
phases.description: Create the @clippyjs/ai-openrouter package structure with all configuration files

subtasks.description: Create package.json for ai-openrouter with correct dependencies and scripts
subtasks.notes: Created package.json for ai-openrouter following the ai-openai pattern. Uses openai SDK as OpenRouter is OpenAI API compatible. Verification passed: package name is @clippyjs/ai-openrouter.

Created tsconfig.json for ai-openrouter package following the exact pattern from ai-openai/tsconfig.json. File extends ../../tsconfig.react.json, references types and ai packages, and includes src/**/* while excluding node_modules and dist.

subtasks.description: Create rollup.config.js for ai-openrouter build configuration
subtasks.notes: Created rollup.config.js for ai-openrouter following ai-openai pattern. External dependencies: @clippyjs/ai and openai. Outputs CJS and ESM formats with sourcemaps.

subtasks.description: Create vitest.config.ts for ai-openrouter test configuration
subtasks.notes: Created vitest.config.ts for ai-openrouter following the exact pattern from ai-openai. File includes globals: true, node environment, and v8 coverage configuration.

phases.name: XAI Provider Implementation
phases.description: Implement the XAIProvider class with full streaming, tools, and vision support

subtasks.description: Create XAIProvider.ts implementing AIProvider interface with xAI-specific configuration (baseURL: https://api.x.ai/v1, default model: grok-4)
subtasks.notes: Created XAIProvider.ts implementing AIProvider interface with xAI-specific configuration (baseURL: https://api.x.ai/v1, default model: grok-4). Follows OpenAIProvider pattern since xAI uses OpenAI-compatible API. Supports streaming, tool use, and vision capabilities.
Created index.ts with XAIProvider export following ai-openai pattern. Verification passed (grep count = 1).

subtasks.description: Create XAIProvider.test.ts with comprehensive unit tests
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive XAIProvider.test.ts with 686 lines of tests covering initialization, model management, feature support, message conversion, tool conversion, streaming, chat options, and cleanup. Tests follow the exact pattern from OpenAIProvider.test.ts. Note: Tests cannot run until yarn install is executed to update lockfile with new workspace packages.

phases.name: OpenRouter Provider Implementation
phases.description: Implement the OpenRouterProvider class with full streaming, tools support, and optional headers

subtasks.description: Create OpenRouterProvider.ts implementing AIProvider interface with OpenRouter-specific configuration (baseURL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1, model format: provider/model-name)
subtasks.notes:
Created OpenRouterProvider.ts implementing AIProvider interface with OpenRouter-specific configuration:
- baseURL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
- Default model: openai/gpt-4o (provider/model-name format)
- HTTP-Referer and X-Title header support
- Dual-mode operation (direct SDK and proxy mode)
- Streaming chat with tool use support
- Vision detection for common vision-capable models
- API key format validation warning
Committed as b237f9b
Created index.ts exporting OpenRouterProvider following the pattern from ai-openai package. Verification passed (1 match found).

subtasks.description: Create OpenRouterProvider.test.ts with comprehensive unit tests
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive OpenRouterProvider.test.ts with 860 lines of tests following the OpenAIProvider.test.ts pattern. Tests cover initialization, model management, feature support, message conversion, tool conversion, streaming, proxy mode, and cleanup. Tests require yarn install to update lockfile before running.

phases.name: Build Verification
phases.description: Install dependencies, build both packages, and verify outputs

verification.command: cd packages/ai-xai && yarn install --check-files 2>&1 | tail -1 && cd ../ai-openrouter && yarn install --check-files 2>&1 | tail -1

subtasks.notes: Dependencies installed successfully. yarn.lock updated with new packages. Both @clippyjs/ai-xai and @clippyjs/ai-openrouter have their workspace dependencies (@clippyjs/ai, @clippyjs/types) and openai SDK properly installed.

subtasks.description: Build ai-xai package and verify output files

verification.command: cd packages/ai-xai && yarn build && test -f dist/index.js && test -f dist/index.esm.js && test -f dist/index.d.ts && echo 'OK'

subtasks.notes: Build verification successful. All required output files generated: dist/index.js, dist/index.esm.js, dist/index.d.ts. Build completed in 949ms with only a minor warning about composite tsconfig setting in referenced project.

subtasks.description: Build ai-openrouter package and verify output files

verification.command: cd packages/ai-openrouter && yarn build && test -f dist/index.js && test -f dist/index.esm.js && test -f dist/index.d.ts && echo 'OK'

subtasks.notes: Build verification successful. All required output files are present in dist/: index.js, index.esm.js, index.d.ts, plus OpenRouterProvider.d.ts and source maps. Build command: npm run build (rollup -c) completed successfully.

subtasks.description: Run TypeScript type checking for both packages

verification.command: cd packages/ai-xai && yarn typecheck && cd ../ai-openrouter && yarn typecheck && echo 'OK'

subtasks.notes: TypeScript type checking passes for both ai-xai and ai-openrouter packages. Required adding composite, declaration, and declarationMap settings to packages/ai/tsconfig.json to enable proper project references.

subtasks.description: Run all unit tests for both packages

verification.command: cd packages/ai-xai && yarn test && cd ../ai-openrouter && yarn test && echo 'OK'

subtasks.notes: All unit tests passed successfully. ai-xai: 27 tests passed, ai-openrouter: 39 tests passed. Verification command returned OK.

parallel_groups.reason: No dependencies, different file sets (separate packages)
Both depend on their respective setup phases, different packages

acceptance_criteria: All existing ai-openai tests still pass (no regressions)
Both new providers pass all unit tests
TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors
API keys are not logged or exposed in code

verification_steps.name: XAI Build
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Build completes, dist/ contains index.js, index.esm.js, index.d.ts

verification_steps.name: OpenRouter Build
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Build completes, dist/ contains index.js, index.esm.js, index.d.ts

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk change adding two new packages. Requires unit tests for core functionality and integration verification through builds. Security scanning needed for API key handling patterns.
